title: Research
text:
Our research focuses on understanding how environmental variation and global change influence biodiversity, species movement, and ecosystem functioning across spatial and temporal scales.
We use integrative approaches combining ecology, geospatial technology, and quantitative modelling.

title: Research
text:
Our research focuses on understanding how environmental variation and global change influence biodiversity, species movement, and ecosystem functioning across spatial and temporal scales.
We use integrative approaches combining ecology, geospatial technology, and quantitative modelling.

title: Research
text:
Research Themes

1. Global change ecology
We study how species distribute and move across space and time, and how climate and land cover change reshape these patterns and processes.


2. Movement ecology in human-modified environments
We use satellite tracking and spatial models to study bird and mammal habitat use, migration, and their responses to environmental change.


3. Biodiversity in human-dominated landscapes
We quantify how environmental heterogeneity and its spatiotemporal dynamics shape biodiversity patterns and ecosystem processes.


4. Spatial epidemiology
We study how environmental conditions influence disease patterns in wildlife and humans.

title: Research
text:
Applications
Our work supports:

Biodiversity conservation planning

Migratory species protection

Ecosystem management under climate change

Understanding wildlife disease emergence

title: Si Lab
text:
We study how environmental variation shapes species distributions, movement and biodiversity across space and time using quantitative ecological and geospatial methods.
We aim to understand ecological systems in a changing world and develop evidence-based strategies for biodiversity conservation and sustainable ecosystem management.

title: Si Lab
text:
We study how environmental variation shapes species distributions, movement and biodiversity across space and time using quantitative ecological and geospatial methods.
We aim to understand ecological systems in a changing world and develop evidence-based strategies for biodiversity conservation and sustainable ecosystem management.

title: Si Lab
text:
Research Areas


Movement Ecology
Satellite tracking and spatial modelling of animal movement and migration.


Biodiversity Dynamics
How environmental change shapes species distributions and ecosystem structure.


Global Change Ecology
Ecological responses to climate change, land-use change, and human pressures.

title: Si Lab
text:
Selected Highlights

New methods for spatiotemporal ecological modelling

Large-scale analysis of migratory movement systems

Integration of long-term remote sensing and ecological data

title: Si Lab
text:
Lab Members
Our group includes researchers working on ecology, spatial analysis, and computational modelling.
